commonIssues:
Registration requires both partners to have active, updated Aadhaar cards linked to current mobile numbers for OTP verification. Mismatched details in Aadhaar (spelling, date of birth) can block the application.

How to fix:

  • Update mobile numbers linked to Aadhaar for both partners at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra before applying.

Discrepancies between the wedding invitation card, Aadhaar, and age proof (e.g., 10th marksheet) regarding names or dates of birth are common causes for rejection.

How to fix:

  • Correct any discrepancies in date of birth or names on the IGRSUP Portal using official 10th marksheet or Aadhaar.

Applicants often face issues if they try to register outside their permanent district. Recent rules mandate that the marriage must be registered where at least one partner or their parents reside permanently.

How to fix:

  • Ensure you apply in the district where the marriage took place or where either partner resides permanently, with valid proof.

The process requires two witnesses who must have valid IDs and be physically present, causing issues when witnesses are unavailable.

How to fix:

  • Secure two witnesses with valid UP address proofs (Aadhaar/Voter ID) to accompany you to the office.

Users sometimes report website glitches, slow loading times, and errors during OTP generation or payment processing on the official Integrated Grievance Redressal System of UP (IGRSUP) website.

How to fix:

  • Clear cookies or use an alternate browser. Keep document sizes under the prescribed limit (usually 200KB).

Even after filing online, both partners and two witnesses must visit the Sub-Registrar's office, which can be challenging for working couples.

How to fix:

  • Both partners and witnesses must be physically present at the Sub-Registrar's office for biometric capture.

For marriages solemnized without parental consent, new UP rules (as of June 2025) require the physical presence of the priest, a full video recording of the marriage, and an affidavit from a family member.

How to fix:

  • Provide the required video recording and an affidavit from a family member as mandated by the recent guidelines.

The verification process by authorities can take longer than expected, particularly if documents require further authentication.

How to fix:

  • Track your application on IGRSUP. Visit the office with the printed acknowledgment slip to follow up.

Some applicants rely on unauthorized agents, leading to high fees, incomplete documentation, or fraudulent registrations that get rejected.

How to fix:

  • Apply directly through the official IGRSUP portal to avoid fraud and excessive fees.
